zotero.abstractNote: Transformers have become the de facto standard for sequence modeling. Despite their empirical success, the theoretical understanding of their training dynamics remains limited. In this work, we study the signal propagation in Transformers through the lens of the rank of the attention matrix. We show that, as the depth of the network increases, the attention matrix tends to become low-rank, a phenomenon we term rank collapse. We provide a theoretical analysis of this phenomenon, showing that it is driven by the softmax operation and the residual connections. We further show that rank collapse is related to the vanishing gradient problem and that it can be mitigated by appropriate initialization and normalization techniques. We validate our theoretical findings with extensive experiments on various architectures and datasets.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 35 (NeurIPS 2022)
